edges.description: Impaired triacylglycerol hydrolysis causes lipid-droplet accumulation in muscle.
Skeletal muscle lipid-droplet storage produces the progressive lipid-storage myopathy.
Skeletal muscle lipid storage causes the early prominent upper-limb weakness pattern.
Cardiac muscle lipid storage contributes to cardiomyopathy.
Intramuscular lipid storage corresponds to fatty replacement on muscle imaging.
Progressive muscle lipid storage and myopathy can lead to limb muscle wasting.
Progressive muscle lipid storage impairs motor endurance.

nodes.description: Cardiac muscle involvement; pure or combined cardiomyopathy occurs in many patients.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet: the combination of skeletal myopathy and cardiomyopathy (21/45)

one patient with NLSD-M was
implanted with a cardioverter defibrillator for severe arrhythmias

nodes.description: Increased susceptibility to fatigue is a frequent motor manifestation of NLSDM.
Limb muscle wasting accompanies the progressive lipid-storage myopathy in a subset of patients.

evidence.snippet: HP:0009055 | Generalized limb muscle atrophy | Occasional (29-5%)

nodes.description: Undegraded triacylglycerol accumulates as cytoplasmic lipid droplets in skeletal and cardiac muscle fibers (and leukocytes, producing Jordans' anomaly), driving myopathy and cardiomyopathy.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
NLSDM usually presents skeletal
myopathy, cardiomyopathy and the multiple organs dysfunction

nodes.description: Selective fatty infiltration of muscle (posterior compartment of the legs) is a characteristic muscle-imaging finding.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
Selective muscle fatty infiltration on posterior compartment of
legs

nodes.description: Progressive lipid-storage skeletal myopathy; right upper-limb weakness is often the early and prominent feature.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
NLSDM usually presents skeletal
myopathy, cardiomyopathy and the multiple organs dysfunction

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
A total of 23
mutations were identified including 11 (47.8%) point mutations, eight (34.8%)
deletions

nodes.description: Biallelic PNPLA2 variants reduce adipose triglyceride lipase (ATGL), the rate-limiting enzyme for intracellular triacylglycerol hydrolysis.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy (NLSDM) is a rare
clinical heterogeneous disorder caused by mutations in the patatin-like
phospholipase domain-containing 2 (PNPLA2) gene

nodes.description: Asymmetric, often right-predominant upper-limb weakness as an early prominent feature.

evidence.reference_title: Neutral lipid storage disease with myopathy in China: a large multicentric cohort study.
evidence.snippet:
Right upper limb weakness was the early and
prominent feature in 61.5% of patients
